assessments.status_rationale: Today’s headlines include macro/war-related economic coverage but do not report the Freddie Mac PMMS 30-year fixed-rate level (or any weekly release value). No direct evidence to mark the threshold as hit or missed.
No item in today’s bullets provides MBA Purchase Index (4-week moving average) year-over-year data. Without the weekly index print, the -20% YoY condition cannot be evaluated.
Today’s news bullets do not include a NAR existing-home sales SAAR release or any numeric print that would confirm ≤3.80 million in a given month.
While markets are discussed in connection with the Iran war, the bullets do not state a specific 10-year U.S. Treasury closing yield (nor a close ≥5.25%).
No Treasury auction results (tail and bid-to-cover for 10Y/30Y nominal coupon auctions) are cited in today’s bullets, so the combined condition cannot be verified.
None of today’s items reports the ICE BofA US High Yield OAS closing level; market commentary alone is insufficient to confirm a ≥600 bps close.
No earnings call transcript or SEC filing excerpt is included that mentions helium supply disruption/constraints tied to Qatar by any listed semiconductor manufacturer.
Today’s bullets do not include an announcement by a hyperscaler of a nuclear-power offtake agreement for data centers with contracted capacity ≥300 MW.

Although multiple items address the Iran war and oil/stockpile releases, none provides Kpler/Vortexa/IEA estimates showing Iran crude exports averaging ≥1.0 mb/d below the February 2026 baseline for a continuous 4-week period.
No CPI release details are provided in today’s bullets, and there is no mention of CPI Shelter (SA) running below 3.0% YoY in any monthly print.
